name: Abbott, Edward Rees
motto: "He sat and bleared his eyes with books."
description: Probably has fewer enemies than any other man in the class: always jolly, and always ready to do anyone a favor when he can. Has never killed himself studying yet, but he always weathers the storm at the end of each term. Proudly points to the fact that he was "sampler" one summer at the Anaconda Smelter.

name: Albertson, Maurice Merton
motto: "He will to bed go sober, Falls with the leaf still in October."
description: Hails from Missouri University, but is so quiet you would hardly realize it. Is a hard, earnest worker, and his class records show him to be a good student. As a basketball player he is a little wonder and will be greatly missed on next year's team.

name: Allen, Ernest James
motto: "Mine hours were nice and lucky."
description: So called because his skull had the quality to resist the impact of a sixteen pound shot. Prof. Dean says the accident improved his mathematics fifty per cent. Does much night prowling. Gets much amusement out of Prof. Harris's illustrated lectures.

name: Beach, James Keller
motto: "'Twas only by favor of mine,' quoth he, 'Ye rode so long alive.'"
description: Spends so much of his time with the fair sex that we don't see him often. Knows how to keep on the good side of the professors. Will finish in June and then—we leave that to you.

name: Bingham, Raymond Alexander
motto: "Oh Ye Gods! Render me worthy of this most noble wife!"
description: Once lived in Massachusetts, but four years continuous residence in Missouri has changed him so that you would never suspect it. Is one of our three married men.

name: Blake, True Walter
motto: "'Tis the mind that makes the body rich."
description: Is both student and athlete, and has a record in both that is hard to equal. Has done much to keep both the school and the class to the front. Can always be counted on when you need help. Has proved himself very valuable to the Rollamo Board this year.

name: Boza, Hector José
high school: Escuela de Minas e Ingenieria, Civil, Lima
motto: "Handsome is that handsome does."
description: Silent and well behaved and stands well in his classes. Has a habit of making high grades on his final examinations. Has a dreamy, far-away look and is doubtless thinking of some senorita in Peru.

name: Coaske, Paul Ephraim
motto: "Give him a harp with a thousand strings, And put him up where the angels sing."
description: Is our star violinist. Why he attends a mining school with his musical talent, is more than we can figure. Will probably start a school of music in Richland.

name: Cody, Benjamin Horace
motto: "Wisely and slow; They stumble, that run fast."
description: Comes from the village of St. Joseph. Is a noted mandolin player and tobacco consumer. Is a great favorite among the girls, and has had more cases than any of the fellows. Has now joined the St. James steadies. Is a favorite of Prof. Harris, and will probably be a plumber.

name: Copeland, Robert Nathaniel
motto: "Just think; she'll be eighty-one when I am forty-nine."
description: Comes from "deah old Boston." Spends his time talking to "brother" and keeping McFadden from harm. Has almost learned to say "here" instead of "heah". Has both talent and ability, but his unconcerned disposition causes many to think him lazy.

name: Detweiler, Milan Harrison
motto: "Books can not always please, however good; Minds are not ever craving for their food."
description: Is of the class of 1910. Was one year in the Illinois coal fields and will now finish with a good class. Nevertheless, we are glad to have him with us for he is a good scout. Is a good athlete and will be missed next year.

name: Elmore, Carlos
high school: Escuela de Minas e Ingenieria Civil, Lima
motto: "The art of a thing is, first its aim, and next, its manner of accomplishment."
description: Is an all-round artist, including sculpting, drawing, and music. His Spanish love songs never fail to make a hit. Spends most of his time talking about the pretty girls and playing the guitar.

name: Engleman, Edward William
motto: "Remember this: That very little is needed to make a happy life."
description: Does not like to own up now that he ever was a "swamp angel." His fame as an actor and comedian reaches as far as Salem. Is a baseball star, and the loss of him and his big stick will be a blow to the baseball team.

name: Flynn, Frank James
motto: "Society is one polished horde, Formed of two mighty tribes: The bores and the bored."
description: "Mickey" and his Irish wit were the life of the class when a Freshman. But now he is as sober and sedate as a judge, perhaps the result of feminine influences. His outbreaks are now confined to the Miners Minstrel, where he is a star attraction.

name: Forrester, David Lawton
motto: "They have looked each other in the eyes And there they found no fault."
description: "Red" takes more interest in student activities than any other man in school, unless it interferes with his St. James fussing. Was one of the minstrel stars until he retired this year. Is a good scout, well liked, and his beacon light will be missed by all.

name: Garcia, Germin
motto: "There was language in his very gesture."
description: Another one of our guests who traveled a long way to come to school here. Loves to talk of Mexico and the pretty girls. Thinks that Mexico could lick the United States if it wasn't for Texas. Is the only one that ever succeeded in causing McVey to waste enough energy to get mad.

name: Hirdler, Eva Endurance
motto: "She needs no eulogy, she speaks for herself."
description: "Little Eva," according to the St. Louis newspapers, is the only female mining engineer in the world. Has been with us two years and says she is sorry that it has not been four. Puts great dependence in her unlimited amount of nerve, and expects to operate a gold mine in California.

name: James, Ivory Small
motto: "Society is no comfort to one not sociable."
description: After trying schools both East and West, he decided that M. S. M. was the only one. We suspect, however, that his stay here is caused by other attractions than the quality of the school.

name: Kurz, Jacob Adolph
motto: "I pray thee, cease thy counsel, which falls into mine ears, As profitless as water in a sieve."
description: How he lives, we don't know, but this is no patent medicine advertisement. Is the giraffe of the basketball team. Says the faculty likes to flunk athletes.

name: Lindau, Sam Paul
motto: "I would rather exceed others in knowledge than in power."
description: Paul comes from William Jewell College but says not to blame him for it, for his parents drove him to it. Is very popular with the younger set whom he captivates with his solos on the bass viol.

name: Lunak, Otto Allen
motto: "A man's character depends on the conditions."
description: Otto is the Grubstaker's leading politician, and vainly tries to fill the place of "Red" Barrett. Was quite a fusser until the arrival of the mighty Kayser. Now spends most of his time with the affairs of the Athletic Association.

name: McFadden, Edwin Cook
motto: "The man who smokes thinks like a sage and acts like a Samaritan."
description: Has two main faults: falling down and telling how things were done at Houghton. Is getting over the latter, but we fear the first will never be overcome.

name: McGoughran, James Edward
motto: "Wherefore he called to his wife, and fled to fashion his work anew."
description: After spending fourteen years in practical work, "Mac" decided that he needed a degree from M. S. M. He leaves us this year not only with a degree but a wife and family as well. Has been a hard worker and carries away with him the respect of everyone.

name: Macomber, Sumner Cooley
motto: "He was a man, take him for all in all, I shall never look upon the like of him again."
description: "Mac" is undoubtedly the best athlete that ever came to M. S. M., and his departure will leave a hole in the athletic department that will be hard to fill. Is a jolly, happy-go-lucky fellow, fearing no one, not even the professors. His only intemperance is drinking buttermilks, for which he holds the school record.

name: Christian, R. Miller, Jr.
motto: "His conduct still right with his argument wrong."
description: You can hear him long before you see him. Worked one year in Butte and claims to be an authority on anything in that district, from "mucking" to geology. Likes to argue with the profs. when anything concerning Butte is mentioned.

name: Robert, Bruce Mitchell
motto: "Thinking that nothing was done, if anything remained to do."
description: "Mitch" originally hails from Illinois and has a big variety of stories and songs that everyone has heard more than once. Is a good student and a musician of note. When anything is to be done for the school, you can count on "Mitch."

name: Harry, William Lee Porth
motto: "Men are but children of a larger growth."
description: "Babe" is the pride of Kansas City. Is a good student and athlete. Is one of the originators of the Hook-worm Society. Has been out in practice one year and is so fond of practical work that he has been known to work for nothing. Is from the class of 1910.

name: Arthur, Gustav Pudewa
motto: "Thou hast no sorrow in thy song, No winter in thy year."
description: "Pudy" proudly proclaims the fact that he is from the "Windy City." Was a good student until this year, but now spends one-half of his time at Powell's and the other half telling the fellows what a good time he is having.

name: Shiv, Raj
motto: "I have often regretted my speech, but never my silence."
description: Shiv comes all the way from India to attend our school, and says that it is well worth the trouble. His chief enjoyment is bothering Macomber, who loves him like a brother.

name: Randolph, Oscar Alan
motto: "In the spring a young man's fancy lightly turns to thoughts of love."
description: "Randy" loves to dream that he is the gun the professors think he is. Outside of this he does nothing else but fuss, being one of the silent kind that get theirs by the use of gum drops. Has found time this year between his frequent visits on Salem Avenue to get together ten jokes for the Rollamo. Is thinking of taking up millinery when he graduates.

name: Smith, Duncan Slater
motto: "A woman spoke and he heeded her not."
description: "Dunc" is naturally a bright fellow and spends his time priming fellows for finals which they pass and he cons. He should have graduated years ago, but showed his wisdom by taking a few years of practical experience and graduating with the class of 1911.

name: Sprague, Roy Elliot
motto: "Fire is the test of gold, adversity of strong men."
description: "Jack" is a quiet fellow whom we have never been able to understand, but has many good points that we know. Is a hard worker, and a good student. We all wish him good luck for we know that he deserves it.

name: Tedrow, Harvey Louis
motto: "A little nonsense now and then, Is relished by the best of men."
description: "Harvey" is the smallest man in the class, but makes up for it in good fellowship. Is a good mixer and, of late, has proven himself to be a good student: even the Director pats him on the shoulder and says, "Harvey, I am proud of you." Is one of the minstrel stars that will be hard to replace. Has only been with us three years, but we wish that it had been more. Has some reputation as a railroader.

name: Townsend, Frank Edgar
motto: "Better wear out than rust out."
description: "Old Faithful," our class president, gets his by work, pure and simple, and has none of those qualities that make the professors think that he knows more than he really does. Is a noted orator and generally takes up more time introducing the Y. M. C. A. speaker than the time of the lecture.

name: Walker, John Perry
motto: "As we advance in life, We learn the limits of our abilities."
description: "Professor," after ten years of practical experience, came back to M. S. M. to get his degree with the class of 1911. He spends his spare time guiding the Sophs along the stony path called Qualitative. Is popular alike with students and faculty.
